ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn term exists for Bánh Mì or Vietnamese meatball sandwich. FOODON_00002473 (sandwich food product) used as closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP (Sandwiches) is the closest parent category. No specific code exists for Bánh Mì with pork meatball filling.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific Vietnamese-style meatball sandwich product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Bánh Mì Xíu Mại sandwich weighing approximately 220 g, comprising a Vietnamese-style baguette filled with pork meatballs in sauce, pork pâté, mayonnaise, pickled vegetables, cilantro, and chilli.

nutrient_source.title: Banh Mi with Pork and Condiments — estimated from USDA FoodData Central component analysis: French bread (FDC ID 325871), Pork meatballs cooked (FDC ID 167903 adapted), Mayonnaise (FDC ID 172741), Pork liver pâté (FDC ID 174609), Pickled vegetables (FDC ID 170071), Cilantro and chilli (minor contributors)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for Bánh Mì Xíu Mại as a composite dish. Nutrient values estimated by weighted component analysis using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods entries for each major ingredient. Approximate composition by weight: baguette 90 g, pork meatball with sauce 80 g, pâté 15 g, mayonnaise 15 g, pickled vegetables 15 g, herbs and chilli 5 g. Per-100 g values derived from this 220 g reference serving. HPB Singapore Foodo and FAO/INFOODS SEA databases were checked; no direct entry for this dish was found.

gi_source.title: White bread / French baguette GI reference — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Bánh Mì Xíu Mại. GI estimated at 68 based on the dominant carbohydrate vehicle being a white wheat baguette (GI ~70 per University of Sydney GI Database for white bread/French baguette). The protein, fat, and fibre from meatball, pâté, and mayonnaise components lower the effective GI slightly from the pure bread value. GI 68 (medium) is a conservative analogous estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (68 × 58.3) / 100 ≈ 39.6 g (carbohydrate per 220 g serving = 26.5 × 2.2 = 58.3 g).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100 g; ~1144 mg per sandwich serving) from pork pâté, mayonnaise, meatball sauce, and pickled vegetables
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of this sandwich due to its high sodium load (~1144 mg per serving, approximately 50% of the 2300 mg daily limit), and should request reduced-sauce and reduced-pâté preparation where possible.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate-to-high glycemic load (~39.6 g GL per serving) driven by refined white wheat baguette as the primary carbohydrate source
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that the white baguette base contributes a significant glycemic load (~39.6 per serving); portion control, pairing with a low-GI side, or choosing a wholegrain bread variant can help moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~3.4 g per 100 g; ~7.5 g per serving) from pork meatball, pork pâté, and mayonnaise, combined with high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this sandwich to occasional consumption given its combined saturated fat (~7.5 g per serving) and high sodium (~1144 mg per serving), both of which are established risk factors for adverse cardiovascular outcomes.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pork Meatball Bánh Mì Sandwich (Bánh Mì Xíu Mại) is a Vietnamese-origin filled baguette sandwich served in Singapore, most notably at Co Chung Restaurant on Lorong Telok. The dish features a crispy, airy Vietnamese-style French baguette filled with braised pork meatballs (xíu mại) in a savoury tomato-based sauce, homemade pork liver pâté spread, homemade mayonnaise, pickled daikon and carrot, fresh cilantro, and sliced fresh chilli. The combination of textures — crunchy bread crust, tender meatballs, creamy pâté and mayo, and crisp pickled vegetables — is characteristic of the bánh mì genre. Nutritionally, the sandwich is energy-dense (~248 kcal per 100 g; ~546 kcal per 220 g serving), with moderate protein (~10.8 g/100 g), moderate fat (~11.2 g/100 g), and significant carbohydrate (~26.5 g/100 g) from the baguette. Sodium content is high (~520 mg/100 g) due to the pâté, sauce, and pickled components, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic load per serving is substantial (~39.6) owing to the refined white baguette base, which is relevant for Type 2 Diabetes management. The dish reflects the Vietnamese culinary heritage of the bánh mì tradition, adapted for the Singapore food scene, and is a popular lunch and light dinner option.
common_user_queries: What is Bánh Mì Xíu Mại?
What is in a pork meatball banh mi?
How many calories in a meatball banh mi sandwich?
Is banh mi high in sodium?
Is Bánh Mì Xíu Mại suitable for diabetics?
Where to buy meatball banh mi in Singapore?
What allergens are in a pork meatball banh mi?
What is the glycemic index of banh mi?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry (HPB Singapore Foodo, FAO/INFOODS SEA, USDA) exists for Bánh Mì Xíu Mại as a composite dish. Nutrient values were estimated by weighted component analysis using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for each major ingredient. GI was estimated by analogy to white bread/French baguette from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component analysis; no direct approved-source entry for this composite dish was found in HPB Singapore Foodo or FAO/INFOODS SEA. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA component sources used for this estimation. 3. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reliably reported across all component sources and the contribution is minor. 4. Selenium is -1 as it is not consistently reported in the USDA SR Legacy entries used for the component estimation. 5. GI of 68 is an analogous estimate based on white wheat baguette as the dominant carbohydrate vehicle, per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). 6. The dish is not halal-certified as it contains pork meatball and pork pâté. 7. Serving size of 220 g is an estimate for one standard sandwich as described; actual restaurant portion may vary. 8. Price of SGD 16.48 is preserved from the field survey data source record (GD002710) for reference only and is not used as a nutritional evidence source.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built complete record from scratch. Estimated nutrients via USDA FoodData Central component analysis. Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) with explicit notes. GI estimated by analogy to white baguette from University of Sydney GI Database. All mandatory Singaporean multilingual names populated (English, Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) plus Vietnamese as the dish's origin language. Aliases consistency check performed — all aliases_by_language entries confirmed present in flat aliases list. Ontology match_type values restricted to allowed set (closest_parent, none). All hard gates verified. Score 8/8 achieved on first iteration.
